| Enterprise integrations | ✗               | Limited           | AWS ecosystem | Extensive     |
| Session persistence     | ✗               | ✓                 | ✓             | ✓             |
| Governance/audit        | Manual          | Basic             | AWS-grade     | Enterprise    |
| Cost                    | API usage       | Subscription      | $19-39/mo     | $20/mo+       |

---

## Tool Selection Decision Tree

```
START: What's your primary need?
│
├─► Greenfield project, well-defined tasks, want fresh context
│   └─► Ralph (Huntley's bash loop) ✓
│
├─► Complex brownfield codebase (100K+ lines)
│   ├─► Need structured workflow? → RPI Loop with Ralph
│   ├─► Want spec-driven approach? → AWS Kiro
│   └─► Enterprise requirements? → Factory Droid
│
├─► Enterprise with governance requirements
│   ├─► AWS ecosystem? → Kiro
│   └─► Model-agnostic? → Factory Droid
│
├─► Need session persistence / resume
│   ├─► OpenAI ecosystem? → Codex CLI
│   └─► AWS ecosystem? → Kiro
│
├─► Budget-constrained experimentation
│   └─► Ralph (Huntley's bash loop) with low iteration limits
│
└─► Learning / Understanding the space
    └─► Start with Ralph (Huntley's bash loop) — simplest, most educational
```

---

## Community Implementations

### Official Resources

| Name                    | Link                                                                                               | Description                                  |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| **Claude Code Plugin**  | [anthropics/claude-code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/plugins/ralph-wiggum) | Official Anthropic plugin (currently broken) |
| **how-to-ralph-wiggum** | [ghuntley/how-to-ralph-wiggum](https://github.com/ghuntley/how-to-ralph-wiggum)                    | Geoffrey Huntley's methodology guide         |

### Workarounds for Broken Plugin

| Name              | Link                                                         | Description                                           |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| **dial481/ralph** | [github.com/dial481/ralph](https://github.com/dial481/ralph) | Uses Write tool instead of bash—works after CVE patch |

### Standalone Implementations

| Name                   | Link                                                                                | Description                          |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| **goralph**            | [itsmostafa/goralph](https://github.com/itsmostafa/goralph)                         | Go implementation with auto git push |
| **ralph-orchestrator** | [mikeyobrien/ralph-orchestrator](https://github.com/mikeyobrien/ralph-orchestrator) | Rust orchestrator with 7 AI backends |
| **Goose Ralph Loop**   | [block.github.io/goose](https://block.github.io/goose/docs/tutorials/ralph-loop/)   | Cross-model review implementation    |

---

# Part VIII: Reference

---

## Quick Reference Card

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    RALPH QUICK REFERENCE                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ THE REAL RALPH (Huntley's Original - RECOMMENDED):              │
│   while :; do cat PROMPT.md | claude-code ; done                │
│                                                                 │
│ VALIDATION ORDER:                                               │
│   1. npm test           # Unit tests                            │
│   2. npm run typecheck  # TypeScript                            │
│   3. npm run lint       # Linting                               │
│   4. npm run build      # Build                                 │
│                                                                 │
│ COMPLETION SIGNALS:                                             │
│   <promise>PLAN_COMPLETE</promise>      # Planning done         │
│   <promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise> # Building done         │
│   <promise>STUCK</promise>              # Cannot proceed        │
│                                                                 │
│ FILE STRUCTURE:                                                 │
│   PROMPT_plan.md         # Planning instructions                │
│   PROMPT_build.md        # Building instructions                │
│   AGENTS.md              # Operational guide (~60 lines)        │
│   IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md # Task tracking                        │
│   specs/*.md             # One file per topic                   │
│                                                                 │
│ TASKMASTER INTEGRATION:                                         │
│   .taskmaster/docs/prd.txt    # PRD source                      │
│   .taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json # Structured tasks               │
│                                                                 │
│ GOLDEN RULES:                                                   │
│   ✓ Fresh context per iteration (Huntley's approach)            │
│   ✓ One task per iteration                                      │
│   ✓ Always use PORT=0 (dynamic ports)                           │
│   ✓ Always run in git-tracked directory                         │
│   ✓ Set --max-iterations (safety net)                           │
│   ✓ Clear completion criteria                                   │
│   ✓ Backpressure over direction                                 │
│   ✓ Plans are disposable—regenerate don't salvage               │
│                                                                 │
│ SECURITY (CRITICAL):                                            │
│   ⚠ Always run in sandbox (Docker/VM)                           │
│   ⚠ Use dedicated API keys with spend limits                    │
│   ⚠ Never run on production systems                             │
│   ⚠ Audit git history after sessions                            │
│   ⚠ Implement Principal Skinner for production                  │
│                                                                 │
│ CLAUDE CODE 2.1.12 (Latest):                                    │
│   ✓ Opus 4.5 available for Pro users                            │
│   ✓ Custom keybindings (~/.claude/keybindings.json)             │
│   ✓ MCP tool auto-search enabled by default                     │
│   ✓ Lifecycle hooks for Principal Skinner patterns              │
│                                                                 │
│ COST ESTIMATION (Opus 4.5):                                     │
│   10 iterations ≈ $5-15 (90% CI)                                │
│   20 iterations ≈ $15-40 (90% CI)                               │
│   50 iterations ≈ $50-150 (90% CI)                              │
│                                                                 │
│ CONTEXT ZONES:                                                  │
│   0-40%   = Smart Zone (optimal quality)                        │
│   40-60%  = Dumb Zone (degraded quality)                        │
│   60-100% = Danger Zone (high failure risk)                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Glossary

| Term                       | Definition                                                                           |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **AFK**                    | Away From Keyboard—running Ralph overnight without supervision                       |
| **Backpressure**           | Engineering automatic rejection of incorrect outputs (tests, linting, type checks)   |
| **Brownfield**             | Existing codebase with legacy code, established patterns, and technical debt         |
| **Compaction**             | Summarizing accumulated context to reduce token usage                                |
| **Completion Promise**     | A specific string (e.g., `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`) that signals task completion |
| **Context Degradation**    | Quality reduction as token accumulation increases (~40% utilization)                 |
| **Context Engineering**    | Building dynamic systems for optimal LLM context management                          |
| **Dumb Zone**              | Context utilization range (~40-60%) where reasoning quality degrades                 |
| **Fresh Context**          | A new Claude instance with no prior conversation history                             |
| **HITL**                   | Human In The Loop—supervised operation                                               |
| **Intentional Compaction** | Deliberately summarizing agent work before handoff (RPI pattern)                     |
| **Lost in the Middle**     | LLMs struggle with information in the middle of long contexts                        |
| **MCP**                    | Model Context Protocol—standard for LLM tool integration                             |
| **Overbaking**             | Agent continues iterating on impossible task, wasting resources                      |
| **Principal Skinner**      | Supervisor harness that monitors and controls agent behavior                         |
| **PRD**                    | Product Requirements Document                                                        |
| **RPI Loop**               | Research-Plan-Implement workflow with specialized agents                             |
| **Smart Zone**             | Context utilization below ~40% where reasoning quality remains high                  |
| **Stop Hook**              | Mechanism that intercepts exit attempts and re-feeds prompts                         |
| **TaskMaster**             | AI-powered task management system for development workflows                          |

---

## Sources & Citations

### Primary Sources

- Huntley, G. (2025, July 14). _Ralph Wiggum as a "software engineer"_. https://ghuntley.com/ralph/
- Huntley, G. (2026, January 17). _Everything is a Ralph Loop_. https://ghuntley.com/loop/
- Horthy, D. (2026, January). _A Brief History of Ralph_. https://www.humanlayer.dev/blog/brief-history-of-ralph
- Cherny, B. (2026, January 7). _Claude Code 2.1.0 Release_. https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases

### Security

- CVE-2025-54795. https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54795
- Cymulate. (2025, August 4). _InversePrompt_. https://cymulate.com/blog/cve-2025-547954-54795-claude-inverseprompt/
- Secure Trajectories. _Supervising Ralph_. https://securetrajectories.substack.com/p/ralph-wiggum-principal-skinner-agent-reliability

### Context Engineering

- Anthropic. (2025, September 29). _Effective Context Engineering_. https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-context-engineering-for-ai-agents
- Horthy, D. (2025, April). _12-Factor Agents_. https://github.com/humanlayer/12-factor-agents

### Task Management

- Toledano, E. _TaskMaster AI_. https://github.com/eyaltoledano/claude-task-master
- Developer Toolkit. _PRD → Plan → Todo Workflow_. https://developertoolkit.ai/en/shared-workflows/development-workflows/prd-plan-todo/

---

## Changelog

### Version 3.0 (January 23, 2026) — Enhanced Edition

**Major Additions:**

- Complete file templates section (Part V)
- PRD format specification with JSON examples
- tasks.json format specification with schema
- IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md step-by-step creation guide
- AGENTS.md and PROMPT templates
- specs/ directory structure guide
- TaskMaster AI integration guide
- MCP server configuration
- Multi-tool workflow examples

**Updates:**

- Claude Code updated to v2.1.12 (latest)
- Opus 4.5 availability for Pro users noted
- New keybindings and MCP auto-search features
- Updated cost estimates and benchmarks
- Enhanced security considerations
- Expanded glossary

**Corrections:**

- Fixed Claude Code version references
- Updated npm package information
- Clarified tasks.json format variations

### Version 2.0 (January 22, 2026) — Complete Edition

- Initial comprehensive documentation
- Claude Code 2.1.x capabilities section
- Enterprise Compliance section
- Production Risks & Unknown Unknowns section
- Recovery Procedures section

---

## Conclusion

The Ralph Wiggum technique represents a paradigm shift in AI-assisted software development. It transforms AI coding agents from interactive assistants into autonomous "night shift" workers through:

1. **Iterative loops** that embrace failure as feedback
2. **Context engineering** that manages token scarcity
3. **Backpressure mechanisms** that ensure convergence
4. **Fresh context per iteration** that maintains quality
5. **Principal Skinner governance** that ensures reliability
6. **Structured file formats** (PRD, tasks.json, IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md) that enable coordination

The technique's power lies in its simplicity—a bash loop that keeps trying until success. As Geoffrey Huntley notes:

> "The technique is deterministically bad in an undeterministic world."

But that deterministic persistence, combined with proper backpressure and clear completion criteria, achieves results that seemed impossible just months ago.

---

_"Software development is dead. Long live software engineering."_
— Geoffrey Huntley

---

**Document compiled from research across 100+ verified sources, January 2026.**

_This Enhanced Edition includes complete file templates, format specifications, step-by-step guides for creating implementation plans, prd.json/tasks.json documentation, and tool integration guides. All benchmark data includes version stamps for reproducibility._

---

© 2026 | This document is provided for educational purposes. Verify current pricing, model capabilities, and security advisories before production deployment.

# AGENTS.md

## Project Overview
React Native microfrontend migration from `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` to `rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram`. Source code is **PRODUCTION** and **QA-TESTED**. No new manual QA will be performed, no reliable automated tests exist.

## Validation Commands

```bash
# MUST pass before marking any task complete (in this order)
./scripts/validate-migration.sh    # Behavioral change detection
npm run typecheck                   # TypeScript validation
```

## Validation Order
Run in this exact order. ALL must pass:
1. `./scripts/validate-migration.sh` — Detects behavioral changes (HARD FAIL)
2. `npm run typecheck` — TypeScript validation

If validation fails, **fix the issue** before proceeding. Do not document and continue.

## Environment

```bash
# Directories
SOURCE_DIR=~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram
TARGET_DIR=~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services
```

## Critical Patterns

### Store Imports (MANDATORY)
```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT - Always use ~/store alias
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';

// ❌ WRONG - Never use relative paths
import { useRootStore } from '../../store';
```

### Zustand Imports
```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT (new zustand path)
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/react/shallow';

// ⚠️ OLD (update if found)
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
```

### Hook Component Pattern
```
screens/Feature/
├── index.ts      # Logic hook: export useFeatureScreen()
├── view.tsx      # Render: export default FeatureScreen
└── Models/
    └── index.d.ts  # Types
```

## SAFE vs UNSAFE Changes

### SAFE (Allowed)
- Import path adjustments for new directory structure
- `~/store` alias usage (mandatory per style guide)
- Filename typo fixes (`invoives` → `invoices`)
- `zustand/shallow` → `zustand/react/shallow`
- File organization (splitting index.tsx into index.ts + view.tsx)
- Removing unnecessary JSDoc comments
- Adding console.error for debugging (no logic change)

### UNSAFE (Forbidden - Script Will FAIL)
- Function signature changes (parameters, return types)
- Interface/type field additions or removals
- State shape changes
- New computed properties
- Different return values
- Class vs function pattern changes
- Enum value changes
- Any logic changes whatsoever

## Known Gotchas

### Permission → InvoiceCashbackPermission
The rename is intentional. BUT the **behavior must match source**:
- Source uses `PermissionStatus` enum with `status` field
- If target changed to `isLoading/isError` booleans, this is UNSAFE
- Revert to source pattern

### pointsQuantity Field
The `extractPointsQuantityFromParameterization()` function in target **does not exist in source**.
- If this is new logic, it must be removed or isolated
- Verify source behavior before keeping

### Service vs Hook Responsibility
Source: Service updates store directly
Target: Hook updates store
- This architectural change is UNSAFE if it changes when/how state updates occur
- Revert to source pattern

## File Naming
- Components: `PascalCase.tsx`
- Hooks: `use*.ts`
- Services: `*.service.ts`
- Stores: `*.store.ts`
- Models: `*.model.ts`

## Do NOT
- Add new parameters to existing functions
- Change return types
- Add new fields to state interfaces
- Remove fields from state interfaces
- Change enum values
- Add computed properties that don't exist in source
- "Fix bugs" that would change runtime behavior

**Source**: `~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram/`
**Target**: `~/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/`
**Last Updated**: [AGENT WILL UPDATE]

---

## Current Status: 🔴 BEHAVIORAL CHANGES DETECTED

Previous migration introduced behavioral changes. This plan reverts them while preserving style guide compliance where safe.

---

## Phase 1: Critical Reversions (UNSAFE → SAFE)

These tasks revert behavioral changes back to source behavior.

### High Priority — State Shape Changes

- [ ] **TASK-001**: Revert `invoicesCashbackPermission.model.ts` state shape
  - Source: `{ hasRedeemed: boolean, status: PermissionStatus }`
  - Target: `{ hasRedeemed: boolean, isLoading: boolean, isError: boolean }`
  - Action: Restore `PermissionStatus` enum and `status` field
  - Files: `models/invoicesCashbackPermission.model.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-002**: Revert `invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts` to source pattern
  - Source: Uses `setStatus()` action with enum
  - Target: Uses `setLoading()`, `setError()` separate actions
  - Action: Restore source action pattern
  - Also: Fix filename typo (`invoives` → `invoices`)
  - Files: `store/invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-003**: Revert `parameter.model.ts` — remove new logic
  - Source: No `pointsQuantity` field, no `extractPointsQuantityFromParameterization()`
  - Target: Has both
  - Action: Remove added field and function (or move to separate file if needed elsewhere)
  - Files: `models/parameter.model.ts`

- [ ] **TASK-004**: Revert `parameter.store.ts` — remove computed property
  - Source: `setData()` just saves data
  - Target: `setData()` also computes `pointsQuantity`
  - Action: Remove computation, match source exactly
  - Files: `store/parameter.store.ts`

### High Priority — Service Behavior Changes

- [ ] **TASK-005**: Revert `invoicesCashbackPermission.service.ts` to update store
  - Source: Service calls store actions (`setStatus`, `setHasRedeemed`)
  - Target: Service only returns data, doesn't update store
  - Action: Restore store update calls in service

  - Target: Reads `isLoading`/`isError` directly from store

### High Priority — API/Repository Pattern Changes

  - Source: Function exports returning `Promise<AxiosResponse<T>>`
  - Target: Class instance with unwrapped responses
  - Action: Restore function pattern with raw AxiosResponse

  - Target: Class with multiple methods
  - Action: Restore function pattern
  - Files: `repository/invoicesCashbackPermission.repository.ts`

  - Source: `TransactionType` has 3 values, `PointsTransaction` has `status` field
  - Target: `TransactionType` has 4 values (added REFUNDED), `status` field missing
  - Action: Restore source definition exactly
  - Files: `models/extract.model.ts`

  - Source: `getTransactionFromDto()` includes `status: dto.status`
  - Target: `status` field missing from mapping
  - Action: Restore status field mapping

# Migration Task Instructions

## Context

You are migrating code from `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` (source) to `rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram` (target).

**CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:**
- Source code is PRODUCTION and QA-TESTED
- No new manual QA will be performed
- No reliable automated tests exist
- You MUST preserve source behavior exactly

Read these files before starting:
- `AGENTS.md` — Build commands, SAFE/UNSAFE rules, gotchas
- `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` — Current task list and status
- `specs/migration-rules.md` — Detailed rules for what's allowed
- `specs/file-mapping.md` — Source to target file mapping

---

## Your Task

### Step 1: Read Current State
```bash
cat IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
cat AGENTS.md
```

### Step 2: Select ONE Task
- Pick the highest-priority incomplete task from the plan
- Respect dependencies (don't start if blockers aren't done)
- ONE task per iteration — do not combine tasks

### Step 3: Before Any Code Changes

For the selected file pair (source → target):

1. **Read source file completely**
2. **Read target file completely**
3. **Identify ALL differences** (not just the obvious ones)
4. **Classify each difference as SAFE or UNSAFE**

If UNSAFE differences exist:
- Revert target to match source behavior
- Apply only SAFE transformations (import paths, file organization)

### Step 4: Implement

- Make the minimal changes necessary
- Preserve source behavior exactly
- Apply style guide only where it doesn't change behavior
- No "improvements" or "bug fixes"

### Step 5: Validate

Run in this exact order (ALL must pass):

```bash
./scripts/validate-migration.sh
npm run typecheck
```

If validation fails:
- Read the error message carefully
- Fix the issue (usually means reverting a change)
- Run validation again
- Do NOT proceed until both pass

### Step 6: Update State

After validation passes:

1. Mark task complete in `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
2. Add discovered work if any
3. Commit changes:
```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "migrate: [filename] - preserve source behavior"
```

---

## Rules

### What You CAN Do
- Change import paths for new directory structure
- Use `~/store` alias (mandatory)
- Fix filename typos
- Split `index.tsx` into `index.ts` + `view.tsx` (if behavior preserved)
- Update `zustand/shallow` to `zustand/react/shallow`

### What You CANNOT Do
- Add/remove function parameters
- Change return types
- Add/remove interface fields
- Change state shapes
- Add computed properties
- Change enum values
- "Fix" anything that would change runtime behavior

### When In Doubt
If unsure whether a change is SAFE:
- It's UNSAFE
- Keep source behavior
- Document the question in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md

---

## Exit Conditions

### Task Complete, More Remain
Exit normally. The loop will restart you with fresh context.

### All Tasks Complete
```
<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>
```

### Stuck (After 3 Failed Attempts)
```
<promise>STUCK</promise>

Reason: [describe the issue]
File: [which file]
Attempts: [what you tried]
```

### Unsafe Change Required
If completing a task REQUIRES an unsafe change:
```
<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>

File: [which file]
Required Change: [what needs to change]
Why Source Behavior Cannot Be Preserved: [explanation]
```

---

## Validation Script Failures

### "BEHAVIORAL CHANGE DETECTED"
The script found a difference in function signatures, interfaces, or state shapes.
- Read the diff output
- Identify which change caused it
- Revert that change
- Keep only SAFE changes

### "MISSING SOURCE FILE"
The file exists in target but not in source.
- This might be a new feature (acceptable)
- Or a renamed file (check mapping)
- Skip validation for truly new files

### "TYPE MISMATCH"
Interface or type definition differs.
- Revert to source definition exactly
- Import path changes are OK
- Field changes are NOT OK

---

## Remember

1. **Source is truth** — When in doubt, match source exactly
2. **No heroics** — Don't fix bugs, don't improve code
3. **One task at a time** — Fresh context prevents drift
4. **Validate before commit** — Script failures mean STOP and FIX
5. **Document uncertainty** — Better to flag than to guess wrong

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→
     3→# =============================================================================
     4→# loop.sh - Ralph Wiggum Loop Orchestrator
     5→# =============================================================================
     6→#
     7→# Usage:
     8→#   ./loop.sh                    # Run with defaults
     9→#   ./loop.sh --max-iterations 30  # Custom iteration limit
    10→#   ./loop.sh --dry-run           # Show what would run without executing
    11→#
    12→# =============================================================================
    13→
    14→set -e
    15→
    16→# Configuration
    17→MAX_ITERATIONS=${MAX_ITERATIONS:-50}
    18→SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS=${SLEEP_BETWEEN:-2}
    19→LOG_FILE="ralph-session-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log"
    20→
    21→# Parse arguments
    22→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    23→    case $1 in
    24→        --max-iterations)
    25→            MAX_ITERATIONS="$2"
    26→            shift 2
    27→            ;;
    28→        --dry-run)
    29→            DRY_RUN=1
    30→            shift
    31→            ;;
    32→        *)
    33→            echo "Unknown option: $1"
    34→            exit 1
    35→            ;;
    36→    esac
    37→done
    38→
    39→# Colors
    40→RED='\033[0;31m'
    41→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    42→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    43→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    44→NC='\033[0m'
    45→
    46→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    47→# Logging
    48→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    49→
    50→log() {
    51→    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    52→    echo -e "[$timestamp] $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    53→}
    54→
    55→log_phase() {
    56→    echo ""
    57→    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    58→    log "$1"
    59→    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    60→}
    61→
    62→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    63→# Pre-flight Checks
    64→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65→
    66→preflight_checks() {
    67→    log_phase "Pre-flight Checks"
    68→    
    69→    # Check required files exist
    70→    local required_files=("PROMPT.md" "AGENTS.md" "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" "scripts/validate-migration.sh")
    71→    for file in "${required_files[@]}"; do
    72→        if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
    73→            log "${RED}ERROR: Required file missing: $file${NC}"
    74→            exit 1
    75→        fi
    76→    done
    77→    log "${GREEN}✓ All required files present${NC}"
    78→    
    79→    # Check validation script is executable
    80→    chmod +x scripts/validate-migration.sh
    81→    log "${GREEN}✓ Validation script executable${NC}"
    82→    
    83→    # Check claude-code is available
    84→    if ! command -v claude-code &> /dev/null; then
    85→        if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
    86→            log "${RED}ERROR: claude-code not found in PATH${NC}"
    87→            exit 1
    88→        fi
    89→        CLAUDE_CMD="claude"
    90→    else
    91→        CLAUDE_CMD="claude-code"
    92→    fi
    93→    log "${GREEN}✓ Claude command available: $CLAUDE_CMD${NC}"
    94→    
    95→    # Check we're in a git repo
    96→    if [ ! -d ".git" ]; then
    97→        log "${YELLOW}WARNING: Not a git repository. Changes won't be tracked.${NC}"
    98→    else
    99→        log "${GREEN}✓ Git repository detected${NC}"
   100→    fi
   101→    
   102→    # Check source/target directories
   103→    if [ ! -d "$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place" ]; then
   104→        log "${RED}ERROR: Source directory not found${NC}"
   105→        exit 1
   106→    fi
   107→    log "${GREEN}✓ Source directory exists${NC}"
   108→}
   109→
   110→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111→# Main Loop
   112→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113→
   114→run_iteration() {
   115→    local iteration=$1
   116→    
   117→    log_phase "Iteration $iteration / $MAX_ITERATIONS"
   118→    
   119→    # Run Claude with the prompt
   120→    log "Invoking Claude..."
   121→    
   122→    local output
   123→    if [ -n "$DRY_RUN" ]; then
   124→        log "${YELLOW}DRY RUN: Would execute: cat PROMPT.md | $CLAUDE_CMD --dangerously-skip-permissions${NC}"
   125→        output="DRY_RUN_OUTPUT"
   126→    else
   127→        output=$(cat PROMPT.md | $CLAUDE_CMD --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1) || true
   128→    fi
   129→    
   130→    # Log output (truncated)
   131→    echo "$output" | tail -100 >> "$LOG_FILE"
   132→    
   133→    # Check for completion signals
   134→    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>"; then
   135→        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
   136→        log "${GREEN}  ALL TASKS COMPLETE!${NC}"
   137→        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
   138→        return 0
   139→    fi
   140→    
   141→    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>STUCK</promise>"; then
   142→        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
   143→        log "${YELLOW}  AGENT IS STUCK - Manual intervention required${NC}"
   144→        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
   145→        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>STUCK</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
   146→        return 2
   147→    fi
   148→    
   149→    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>"; then
   150→        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
   151→        log "${RED}  UNSAFE CHANGE REQUIRED - Human decision needed${NC}"
   152→        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
   153→        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
   154→        return 3
   155→    fi
   156→    
   157→    # Run validation as additional backpressure
   158→    log "Running validation..."
   159→    if ! ./scripts/validate-migration.sh >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
   160→        log "${YELLOW}Validation failed - next iteration will address${NC}"
   161→    fi
   162→    
   163→    # Continue to next iteration
   164→    return 1
   165→}
   166→
   167→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   168→# Main
   169→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170→
   171→main() {
   172→    log_phase "Ralph Wiggum Loop Starting"
   173→    log "Max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS"
   174→    log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
   175→    
   176→    preflight_checks
   177→    
   178→    local iteration=0
   179→    local exit_code=1
   180→    
   181→    while [ $iteration -lt $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; do
   182→        iteration=$((iteration + 1))
   183→        
   184→        run_iteration $iteration
   185→        exit_code=$?
   186→        
   187→        if [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
   188→            log "Sleeping ${SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS}s before next iteration..."
   189→            sleep $SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS
   190→        fi
   191→    done
   192→    
   193→    log_phase "Loop Finished"
   194→    
   195→    if [ $iteration -ge $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
   196→        log "${YELLOW}Reached maximum iterations ($MAX_ITERATIONS)${NC}"
   197→        log "Review progress and run again if needed."
   198→        exit 1
   199→    fi
   200→    
   201→    case $exit_code in
   202→        0)
   203→            log "${GREEN}Migration completed successfully!${NC}"
   204→            exit 0
   205→            ;;
   206→        2)
   207→            log "${YELLOW}Agent stuck after $iteration iterations.${NC}"
   208→            exit 2
   209→            ;;
   210→        3)
   211→            log "${RED}Unsafe change required. Human decision needed.${NC}"
   212→            exit 3
   213→            ;;
   214→    esac
   215→}
   216→
   217→main "$@"
   218→

#!/bin/bash

# =============================================================================
# loop.sh - Ralph Wiggum Loop Orchestrator
# =============================================================================
#
# Usage:
#   ./loop.sh                    # Run with defaults
#   ./loop.sh --max-iterations 30  # Custom iteration limit
#   ./loop.sh --dry-run           # Show what would run without executing
#
# =============================================================================

set -e

# Configuration
MAX_ITERATIONS=${MAX_ITERATIONS:-50}
SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS=${SLEEP_BETWEEN:-2}
LOG_FILE="ralph-session-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log"

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        --max-iterations)
            MAX_ITERATIONS="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        --dry-run)
            DRY_RUN=1
            shift
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown option: $1"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logging
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

log() {
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    echo -e "[$timestamp] $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_phase() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    log "$1"
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-flight Checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

preflight_checks() {
    log_phase "Pre-flight Checks"
    
    # Check required files exist
    local required_files=("PROMPT.md" "AGENTS.md" "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" "scripts/validate-migration.sh")
    for file in "${required_files[@]}"; do
        if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
            log "${RED}ERROR: Required file missing: $file${NC}"
            exit 1
        fi
    done
    log "${GREEN}✓ All required files present${NC}"
    
    # Check validation script is executable
    chmod +x scripts/validate-migration.sh
    log "${GREEN}✓ Validation script executable${NC}"
    
    # Check claude-code is available
    if ! command -v claude-code &> /dev/null; then
        if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
            log "${RED}ERROR: claude-code not found in PATH${NC}"
            exit 1
        fi
        CLAUDE_CMD="claude"
    else
        CLAUDE_CMD="claude-code"
    fi
    log "${GREEN}✓ Claude command available: $CLAUDE_CMD${NC}"
    
    # Check we're in a git repo
    if [ ! -d ".git" ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}WARNING: Not a git repository. Changes won't be tracked.${NC}"
    else
        log "${GREEN}✓ Git repository detected${NC}"
    fi
    
    # Check source/target directories
    if [ ! -d "$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place" ]; then
        log "${RED}ERROR: Source directory not found${NC}"
        exit 1
    fi
    log "${GREEN}✓ Source directory exists${NC}"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main Loop
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

run_iteration() {
    local iteration=$1
    
    log_phase "Iteration $iteration / $MAX_ITERATIONS"
    
    # Run Claude with the prompt
    log "Invoking Claude..."
    
    local output
    if [ -n "$DRY_RUN" ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}DRY RUN: Would execute: cat PROMPT.md | $CLAUDE_CMD --dangerously-skip-permissions${NC}"
        output="DRY_RUN_OUTPUT"
    else
        output=$(cat PROMPT.md | $CLAUDE_CMD --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1) || true
    fi
    
    # Log output (truncated)
    echo "$output" | tail -100 >> "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Check for completion signals
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>"; then
        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${GREEN}  ALL TASKS COMPLETE!${NC}"
        log "${GREEN}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>STUCK</promise>"; then
        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${YELLOW}  AGENT IS STUCK - Manual intervention required${NC}"
        log "${YELLOW}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>STUCK</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
        return 2
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>"; then
        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        log "${RED}  UNSAFE CHANGE REQUIRED - Human decision needed${NC}"
        log "${RED}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
        echo "$output" | grep -A 10 "<promise>UNSAFE_REQUIRED</promise>" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
        return 3
    fi
    
    # Run validation as additional backpressure
    log "Running validation..."
    if ! ./scripts/validate-migration.sh >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1; then
        log "${YELLOW}Validation failed - next iteration will address${NC}"
    fi
    
    # Continue to next iteration
    return 1
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

main() {
    log_phase "Ralph Wiggum Loop Starting"
    log "Max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS"
    log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    
    preflight_checks
    
    local iteration=0
    local exit_code=1
    
    while [ $iteration -lt $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; do
        iteration=$((iteration + 1))
        
        run_iteration $iteration
        exit_code=$?
        
        if [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
            log "Sleeping ${SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS}s before next iteration..."
            sleep $SLEEP_BETWEEN_ITERATIONS
        fi
    done
    
    log_phase "Loop Finished"
    
    if [ $iteration -ge $MAX_ITERATIONS ] && [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
        log "${YELLOW}Reached maximum iterations ($MAX_ITERATIONS)${NC}"
        log "Review progress and run again if needed."
        exit 1
    fi
    
    case $exit_code in
        0)
            log "${GREEN}Migration completed successfully!${NC}"
            exit 0
            ;;
        2)
            log "${YELLOW}Agent stuck after $iteration iterations.${NC}"
            exit 2
            ;;
        3)
            log "${RED}Unsafe change required. Human decision needed.${NC}"
            exit 3
            ;;
    esac
}

main "$@"

   245→    return 0

   263→    exit 1
   264→fi
   265→
   266→if [ ! -d "$TARGET_DIR" ]; then

   269→fi
   270→

   272→echo ""
   273→

   278→    check_new_additions "$SOURCE_DIR/$source_rel" "$TARGET_DIR/$target_rel" "$target_rel"

   291→    echo "2. Revert target to match source behavior"
   292→    echo "3. Run this script again"
   293→    echo ""
   294→    exit 1
   295→fi
   296→
   297→if [ $WARNINGS_FOUND -gt 0 ]; then
   298→    echo -e "${YELLOW}PASSED with $WARNINGS_FOUND warning(s)${NC}"
   299→else

   301→fi

#!/bin/bash

# =============================================================================
# validate-migration.sh
# Backpressure script for Ralph loop - detects behavioral changes
# =============================================================================

set -e

SOURCE_DIR="${SOURCE_DIR:-$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/src/newArch/store/rewardProgram}"
TARGET_DIR="${TARGET_DIR:-$HOME/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services}"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

ERRORS_FOUND=0
WARNINGS_FOUND=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Utility Functions
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}❌ ERROR: $1${NC}"
    ERRORS_FOUND=$((ERRORS_FOUND + 1))
}

log_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠️  WARNING: $1${NC}"
    WARNINGS_FOUND=$((WARNINGS_FOUND + 1))
}

log_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✅ $1${NC}"
}

log_info() {
    echo -e "   $1"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract TypeScript Signatures
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

extract_interfaces() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Extract interface/type definitions with their fields
        grep -E "^export (interface|type) " "$file" 2>/dev/null | sort || true
    fi
}

extract_function_signatures() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Extract exported function signatures (simplified)
        grep -E "^export (const|function|async function) [a-zA-Z]+" "$file" 2>/dev/null | \
            sed 's/=.*//' | sed 's/{.*//' | sort || true
    fi
}

extract_enum_values() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Extract enum definitions
        grep -E "^export enum " "$file" 2>/dev/null || true
        # Also get enum values (lines inside enums)
        awk '/^export enum /,/^}/' "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^\s+[A-Z_]+" || true
    fi
}

extract_class_methods() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Extract class method signatures
        grep -E "^\s+(public |private |async )?(get|set|[a-z][a-zA-Z]+)\(" "$file" 2>/dev/null | \
            sed 's/{.*//' | sort || true
    fi
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# File Mapping (Source → Target)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

declare -A FILE_MAP=(
    # Models
    ["models/parameter.model.ts"]="models/parameter.model.ts"
    ["models/extract.model.ts"]="models/extract.model.ts"
    ["models/airlineMiles.model.ts"]="models/airlineMiles.model.ts"
    ["models/permission.model.ts"]="models/invoicesCashbackPermission.model.ts"
    
    # API
    ["api/parameter.api.ts"]="api/parameter.api.ts"
    ["api/extract.api.ts"]="api/extract.api.ts"
    ["api/airlineMiles.api.ts"]="api/airlineMiles.api.ts"
    ["api/permission.api.ts"]="api/invoicesCashbackPermission.api.ts"
    
    # Repository
    ["repository/parameter.repository.ts"]="repository/parameter.repository.ts"
    ["repository/extract.repository.ts"]="repository/extract.repository.ts"
    ["repository/permission.repository.ts"]="repository/invoicesCashbackPermission.repository.ts"
    
    # Services
    ["services/parameter.service.ts"]="services/parameter.service.ts"
    ["services/extract.service.ts"]="services/extract.service.ts"
    ["services/airlineMiles.service.ts"]="services/airlineMiles.service.ts"
    ["services/permission.service.ts"]="services/invoicesCashbackPermission.service.ts"
    
    # Store
    ["store/parameter.store.ts"]="store/parameter.store.ts"
    ["store/extract.store.ts"]="store/extract.store.ts"
    ["store/airlineMiles.store.ts"]="store/airlineMiles.store.ts"
    ["store/permission.store.ts"]="store/invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts"
    
    # Hooks
    ["hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts"]="hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts"
    ["hooks/usePermission.ts"]="hooks/useInvoiceCashbackPermission.ts"
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validation Functions
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

validate_interfaces() {
    local source_file="$1"
    local target_file="$2"
    local rel_path="$3"
    
    local source_interfaces=$(extract_interfaces "$source_file")
    local target_interfaces=$(extract_interfaces "$target_file")
    
    if [ "$source_interfaces" != "$target_interfaces" ]; then
        log_error "Interface mismatch in $rel_path"
        echo "  Source interfaces:"
        echo "$source_interfaces" | sed 's/^/    /'
        echo "  Target interfaces:"
        echo "$target_interfaces" | sed 's/^/    /'
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

validate_enums() {
    local source_file="$1"
    local target_file="$2"
    local rel_path="$3"
    
    local source_enums=$(extract_enum_values "$source_file")
    local target_enums=$(extract_enum_values "$target_file")
    
    if [ "$source_enums" != "$target_enums" ]; then
        log_error "Enum mismatch in $rel_path"
        echo "  Source enums:"
        echo "$source_enums" | sed 's/^/    /'
        echo "  Target enums:"
        echo "$target_enums" | sed 's/^/    /'
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

validate_functions() {
    local source_file="$1"
    local target_file="$2"
    local rel_path="$3"
    
    local source_funcs=$(extract_function_signatures "$source_file")
    local target_funcs=$(extract_function_signatures "$target_file")
    
    if [ "$source_funcs" != "$target_funcs" ]; then
        log_error "Function signature mismatch in $rel_path"
        echo "  Source functions:"
        echo "$source_funcs" | sed 's/^/    /'
        echo "  Target functions:"
        echo "$target_funcs" | sed 's/^/    /'
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

validate_file_pair() {
    local source_rel="$1"
    local target_rel="$2"
    
    local source_file="$SOURCE_DIR/$source_rel"
    local target_file="$TARGET_DIR/$target_rel"
    
    # Check if source exists
    if [ ! -f "$source_file" ]; then
        log_warning "Source file not found: $source_rel"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check if target exists
    if [ ! -f "$target_file" ]; then
        log_error "Target file missing: $target_rel (expected from $source_rel)"
        return 1
    fi
    
    local has_error=0
    
    # Validate interfaces
    validate_interfaces "$source_file" "$target_file" "$target_rel" || has_error=1
    
    # Validate enums
    validate_enums "$source_file" "$target_file" "$target_rel" || has_error=1
    
    # Validate function signatures
    validate_functions "$source_file" "$target_file" "$target_rel" || has_error=1
    
    if [ $has_error -eq 0 ]; then
        log_success "$target_rel matches source"
    fi
    
    return $has_error
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Check for New Fields/Properties Not in Source
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

check_new_additions() {
    local source_file="$1"
    local target_file="$2"
    local rel_path="$3"
    
    if [ ! -f "$source_file" ] || [ ! -f "$target_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for fields in target that don't exist in source
    local target_fields=$(grep -E "^\s+[a-zA-Z]+(\?)?:" "$target_file" 2>/dev/null | sed 's/:.*//' | tr -d ' ' | sort -u || true)
    local source_fields=$(grep -E "^\s+[a-zA-Z]+(\?)?:" "$source_file" 2>/dev/null | sed 's/:.*//' | tr -d ' ' | sort -u || true)
    
    local new_fields=$(comm -23 <(echo "$target_fields") <(echo "$source_fields") 2>/dev/null || true)
    
    if [ -n "$new_fields" ]; then
        log_error "New fields in $rel_path not present in source:"
        echo "$new_fields" | sed 's/^/    /'
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main Validation
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "=============================================="
echo "  Migration Validation Script"
echo "=============================================="
echo ""
echo "Source: $SOURCE_DIR"
echo "Target: $TARGET_DIR"
echo ""

# Check directories exist
if [ ! -d "$SOURCE_DIR" ]; then
    log_error "Source directory not found: $SOURCE_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -d "$TARGET_DIR" ]; then
    log_error "Target directory not found: $TARGET_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Validating file pairs..."
echo ""

# Validate each mapped file pair
for source_rel in "${!FILE_MAP[@]}"; do
    target_rel="${FILE_MAP[$source_rel]}"
    validate_file_pair "$source_rel" "$target_rel"
    check_new_additions "$SOURCE_DIR/$source_rel" "$TARGET_DIR/$target_rel" "$target_rel"
done

echo ""
echo "=============================================="
echo "  Validation Summary"
echo "=============================================="

if [ $ERRORS_FOUND -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${RED}FAILED: $ERRORS_FOUND error(s) found${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "Behavioral changes detected. You must:"
    echo "1. Identify which changes caused the failures"
    echo "2. Revert target to match source behavior"
    echo "3. Run this script again"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

if [ $WARNINGS_FOUND -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}PASSED with $WARNINGS_FOUND warning(s)${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${GREEN}PASSED: All validations successful${NC}"
fi

exit 0

1→# Migration Rules Specification
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→This document defines what changes are permitted during the migration from `rnm-app-fintech-card-place` to `rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram`.
     6→
     7→**Core Principle**: Source behavior must be preserved exactly. The source code is production and QA-tested. No new testing will be performed.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## SAFE Changes (Allowed)
    12→
    13→These changes do NOT affect runtime behavior and ARE permitted.
    14→
    15→### 1. Import Path Adjustments
    16→
    17→```typescript
    18→// Source (card-place structure)
    19→import { something } from '../../../../utils';
    20→import { store } from '../../store';
    21→
    22→// Target (rewardprogram structure) - SAFE
    23→import { something } from '../../utils';
    24→import { store } from '~/store';  // Alias required by style guide
    25→```
    26→
    27→**Rule**: Path changes due to different directory structure are SAFE.
    28→
    29→### 2. Store Alias Usage
    30→
    31→```typescript
    32→// Source (relative path)
    33→import { useRootStore } from '../../store';
    34→
    35→// Target (alias) - SAFE and REQUIRED
    36→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
    37→```
    38→
    39→**Rule**: The `~/store` alias is mandatory per style guide. This is SAFE.
    40→
    41→### 3. Zustand Import Path Update
    42→
    43→```typescript
    44→// Source (old path)
    45→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
    46→
    47→// Target (new path) - SAFE
    48→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/react/shallow';
    49→```
    50→
    51→**Rule**: Zustand module reorganization. Same functionality, different path.
    52→
    53→### 4. Filename Typo Fixes
    54→
    55→```
    56→// Source
    57→invoivesCashbackPermission.store.ts  ← typo
    58→
    59→// Target - SAFE
    60→invoicesCashbackPermission.store.ts  ← corrected
    61→```
    62→
    63→**Rule**: Fixing obvious typos in filenames is SAFE. Update all imports accordingly.
    64→
    65→### 5. File Organization (Hook Component Pattern)
    66→
    67→```
    68→// Source (combined file)
    69→screens/Feature/index.tsx  ← contains both logic and render
    70→
    71→// Target (split) - SAFE if behavior identical
    72→screens/Feature/
    73→├── index.ts    ← logic hook only
    74→├── view.tsx    ← render only
    75→└── Models/
    76→    └── index.d.ts
    77→```
    78→
    79→**Rule**: Splitting files is SAFE if:
    80→- All logic moves to hook
    81→- All render moves to view
    82→- Types move to Models
    83→- NO behavioral changes in the process
    84→
    85→### 6. Removing JSDoc Comments
    86→
    87→```typescript
    88→// Source
    89→/**
    90→ * This function does something.
    91→ * @param {string} id - The ID
    92→ * @returns {Promise<Data>} The data
    93→ */
    94→export const getData = (id: string): Promise<Data> => { ... }
    95→
    96→// Target - SAFE
    97→export const getData = (id: string): Promise<Data> => { ... }
    98→```
    99→
   100→**Rule**: Removing JSDoc comments is SAFE. They don't affect runtime.
   101→
   102→### 7. Adding Console Logging
   103→
   104→```typescript
   105→// Source
   106→} catch (error) {
   107→  throw error;
   108→}
   109→
   110→// Target - SAFE (console only)
   111→} catch (error) {
   112→  console.error('getData failed:', error);
   113→  throw error;
   114→}
   115→```
   116→
   117→**Rule**: Adding `console.log/warn/error` is SAFE. It doesn't change behavior.
   118→
   119→### 8. Whitespace and Formatting
   120→
   121→```typescript
   122→// Any formatting changes that don't affect logic are SAFE
   123→```
   124→
   125→**Rule**: Prettier/ESLint formatting changes are SAFE.
   126→
   127→---
   128→
   129→## UNSAFE Changes (Forbidden)
   130→
   131→These changes AFFECT runtime behavior and are NOT permitted.
   132→
   133→### 1. Function Signature Changes
   134→
   135→```typescript
   136→// Source
   137→export const checkHasRedeemed = (params: { forceRefresh?: boolean }) => { ... }
   138→
   139→// UNSAFE - New parameter added
   140→export const checkHasRedeemed = (params: { showLoading?: boolean; forceRefresh?: boolean }) => { ... }
   141→```
   142→
   143→**Rule**: NEVER add, remove, or modify function parameters.
   144→
   145→### 2. Return Type Changes
   146→
   147→```typescript
   148→// Source
   149→getPermissionApi(): Promise<AxiosResponse<PermissionDocumentResponse>>
   150→
   151→// UNSAFE - Different return type
   152→getPermission(): Promise<{ data: PermissionDocument }>
   153→```
   154→
   155→**Rule**: Return types must match source exactly.
   156→
   157→### 3. Interface Field Changes
   158→
   159→```typescript
   160→// Source
   161→interface PermissionState {
   162→  hasRedeemed: boolean;
   163→  status: PermissionStatus;
   164→}
   165→
   166→// UNSAFE - Different fields
   167→interface InvoiceCashbackPermissionState {
   168→  hasRedeemed: boolean;
   169→  isLoading: boolean;  // NOT in source
   170→  isError: boolean;    // NOT in source
   171→}
   172→```
   173→
   174→**Rule**: Interface fields must match source exactly.
   175→
   176→### 4. State Shape Changes
   177→
   178→```typescript
   179→// Source (uses enum)
   180→rewardsPermission: {
   181→  hasRedeemed: boolean,
   182→  status: PermissionStatus.IDLE | LOADING | SUCCESS | ERROR
   183→}
   184→
   185→// UNSAFE - Different shape
   186→invoiceCashbackPermission: {
   187→  hasRedeemed: boolean,
   188→  isLoading: boolean,
   189→  isError: boolean
   190→}
   191→```
   192→
   193→**Rule**: State shapes must match source exactly.
   194→
   195→### 5. Enum Value Changes
   196→
   197→```typescript
   198→// Source
   199→type TransactionType = 'CREDIT' | 'RESCUE' | 'EXPIRED';
   200→
   201→// UNSAFE - New value added
   202→type TransactionType = 'CREDIT' | 'RESCUE' | 'EXPIRED' | 'REFUNDED';
   203→```
   204→
   205→**Rule**: Enum/union values must match source exactly.
   206→
   207→### 6. New Computed Properties
   208→
   209→```typescript
   210→// Source (setData just saves)
   211→setData: (data) => set({ parameterPointsProgram: { ...state, data } })
   212→
   213→// UNSAFE - Adds computation
   214→setData: (data) => set({ 
   215→  parameterPointsProgram: { 
   216→    ...state, 
   217→    data,
   218→    pointsQuantity: extractPointsQuantity(data)  // NOT in source
   219→  } 
   220→})
   221→```
   222→
   223→**Rule**: Do not add computed properties that don't exist in source.
   224→
   225→### 7. Responsibility Shifts
   226→
   227→```typescript
   228→// Source - Service updates store
   229→export const checkHasRedeemed = async () => {
   230→  setStatus(PermissionStatus.LOADING);
   231→  const result = await api.get();
   232→  setHasRedeemed(result);
   233→  setStatus(PermissionStatus.SUCCESS);
   234→}
   235→
   236→// UNSAFE - Service doesn't update store (moved to hook)
   237→export const hasRedeemed = async () => {
   238→  const result = await api.get();
   239→  return result;  // No store update
   240→}
   241→```
   242→
   243→**Rule**: Keep responsibilities where source has them.
   244→
   245→### 8. Logic Changes
   246→
   247→```typescript
   248→// Source
   249→const isRescueConcluded = !isRescueFailed && !!rescueReducer?.status;
   250→
   251→// UNSAFE - Different logic
   252→const isRescueConcluded = rescueStatus === RescueStatus.SUCCESS && !!rescueResponse;
   253→```
   254→
   255→**Rule**: Logic must be identical to source.
   256→
   257→### 9. Class vs Function Pattern Changes
   258→
   259→```typescript
   260→// Source - Function exports
   261→export const getPermissionApi = () => api.get('/permission');
   262→export const savePermissionApi = (data) => api.post('/permission', data);
   263→
   264→// UNSAFE - Class pattern
   265→class PermissionApi {
   266→  getPermission() { return api.get('/permission'); }
   267→  savePermission(data) { return api.post('/permission', data); }
   268→}
   269→export const permissionApi = new PermissionApi();
   270→```
   271→
   272→**Rule**: Keep the same pattern as source.
   273→
   274→---
   275→
   276→## Decision Matrix
   277→
   278→| Change Type | SAFE? | Reason |
   279→|-------------|-------|--------|
   280→| Import paths | ✅ | Different directory structure |
   281→| `~/store` alias | ✅ | Style guide requirement |
   282→| zustand import path | ✅ | Library reorganization |
   283→| Filename typos | ✅ | Obvious corrections |
   284→| Hook Component split | ✅ | If behavior preserved |
   285→| Remove JSDoc | ✅ | No runtime effect |
   286→| Add console.log | ✅ | No behavior change |
   287→| Add parameter | ❌ | Changes function signature |
   288→| Remove parameter | ❌ | Changes function signature |
   289→| Change return type | ❌ | Changes contract |
   290→| Add interface field | ❌ | Changes state shape |
   291→| Remove interface field | ❌ | Changes state shape |
   292→| Add enum value | ❌ | Changes type domain |
   293→| Change logic | ❌ | Changes behavior |
   294→| Add computed property | ❌ | New behavior |
   295→| Shift responsibility | ❌ | Changes when things happen |
   296→
   297→---
   298→
   299→## When In Doubt
   300→
   301→If you're unsure whether a change is SAFE:
   302→
   303→1. **Assume it's UNSAFE**
   304→2. **Keep source behavior exactly**
   305→3. **Document the question** in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md under "Human Decisions Required"
   306→4. **Do not proceed** with the uncertain change
   307→
   308→Better to be too conservative than to break production.
   309→
